message:
feat(reports): Cx-first generate_session_report (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Report generation reads 5 storage queries to produce a
multi-section Markdown session summary. On large DBs this
can take noticeable time — a natural fit for cx checkpoints
between sections so operators can cancel a slow report
cleanly.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `generate_session_report_with_cx(cx, storage, opts)` —
    Cx-first parallel of `generate_session_report`. 4
    checkpoint seams (pre-flight, between events/workflows,
    between workflows/gaps, between gaps/audit). Mid-
    generation cancellation surfaces with the pending section
    name in the error message.

Partial-output semantics: on cancellation, the partial
Markdown String is abandoned. A half-built report would be
misleading; returning Err forces the caller to retry on a
fresh cx.

Regression test `report_empty_db_with_cx_matches_legacy`
asserts the two Markdown strings are BIT-FOR-BIT EQUAL on
empty DB (footer is static `ft v{VERSION}` so byte-equality
is achievable — stronger than field-shape parity).

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * reports:: — 47 passed / 0 failed
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (36.40s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
